INTRODUCTION
The state of the art of site inspections has evolved from traditional methods to intelligent safety monitoring.The Age of Traditional Site Inspections to Intelligent Safety Monitoring.
The Evolution from Traditional Site Inspections to Intelligent Safety Monitoring
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcRiwVMovBfYqRUjGYIb6cKBuuL2DBxuPDgGMJ6pM6iefw&s=10The skyline of each modern city is a very interesting story. The residential towers, offices, smart townships, industrial parks and mixed use development are result of years of planning and engineering brilliance and huge investments. However, one issue has always remained consistent for all the successful real estate developments in the past few decades; construction site safety. Thousands of people are at the worksite every day working with heavy equipment, working at heights, handling hazardous materials and working on several different jobs at once. Construction is one of the most hazardous industries in the world, despite the fact that it has an experienced supervisor and safety regulations, the risks in construction change every second. The traditional safety monitoring is based on manual inspection, manual observation and periodical observation, it is not easy to detect the hazard in time before it becomes an accident. The ever-growing challenge has brought about a technology that has revolutionized the construction and real estate industry: Computer Vision with Artificial Intelligence (AI).
Understanding Computer Vision in Construction Safety
What is Computer Vision❓
Computer Vision is used to allow machines to perceive and understand visual information in much the same way that a human does, except that it does not get tired, distracted or overwhelmed. Rather than relying upon safety officers to stroll around the site a handful of times per day, the AI-powered cameras constantly track each and every critical space, pinpointing unsafe conditions within seconds of them occurring. Recent advances in Computer Vision technology have taken it beyond the role of merely being a surveillance camera. The modern systems use intelligent algorithms, neural networks, drones, CCTV networks, IoT, Building Information Modeling (BIM), and cloud computing to create an intelligent ecosystem with the ability to recognize objects, track the movements of workers, detect safety violation, monitor equipment and generate alerts before any incidents. Such systems are not meant for investigating accidents in the future, but are intended to prevent them.
Why Computer Vision Is a Business Investment
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcSFeuw5npvo85uRP64n4Z5EhajQ1H92XyorGK1ryRtB8g&s=10This technological revolution isn’t only a shift in how projects are managed, but can also make a huge difference for builders and developers with big residential or commercial projects as it relates to profitability. When accidents slow construction work, it disrupts schedules, costs more to insure, puts a company under regulatory scrutiny, decreases productivity among workers, and tarnishes a company’s reputation. While returns are always a part of investor consideration, environmental, social and governance (ESG) factors are also gaining momentum and worker safety is one of the major factors being taken into account when evaluating responsible project management. A company’s proactivity in leveraging AI for safety monitoring carries implications beyond just the technical aspects and resonates with investors, financial institutions, government authorities, and potential clients, all of whom are being seen as attentive to risk management, technology, and sustainability. So Computer Vision is not another digital tool, it’s a business investment.
Major Applications of Computer Vision in Construction Safety
PPE Compliance Monitoring
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcR4u0lT8JyW0Gk58D32nQc4dD59iK9EgKv3vB-xTzNU_g&s=10The most useful applications of Computer Vision include Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) compliance monitoring. How can manual supervision be a solution to safety when it comes to ensuring compliance for hundreds of workers on construction sites who are required to wear safety helmets, reflective jackets, gloves, harnesses, goggles and protective footwear? AI technology cameras can automatically detect when each individual that enters a work zone is wearing the correct equipment. If a worker is not wearing a helmet, a safety vest or in a restricted area without permission, the system will automatically alert supervisors via dashboards and mobile messaging. This immediate response reduces the response time to a hazard and corrective action and offers a safer working environment, and decreases the need for continual human watch.
Automated Hazard Detection and Risk Identification
Press enter or click to view image in full size

https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcT_Dp1c8AGbnRIyHmx2B0c5Y1AIJgD68y16ZqI5YReciQ&s=10Another of the incredible options is auto hazard identification. While scaffolding is being erected or dismantled, excavations go on, the machines come in and out, materials are transported and the weather changes.Materials come and go, machines roll in and out, the wind shifts and as scaffolding sets up or comes down, and excavation goes on, minute by minute, working a construction site. Such fluid surroundings pose a severe problem for the humans to evaluate risk. Computer Vision algorithms are used to detect unsafe working postures, unauthorised entry into hazardous zones, proximity between heavy machinery and workers, overcrowded working zones, unstable material storage and abnormal working patterns in real-time, watching live video streams. Project managers receive site status information as it happens, rather than having to make site visits, so they can take action before minor issues become major expenditure through accidents. This shift is seen as the shift from reactive safety management to predictive construction safety by researchers and industry practitioners.
AI-Powered Drone Surveillance for Construction Sites
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcSn8rf5_KoALkodxw5kEb1x88J3VUY2eM7Si3PifxDDEg&s=10In today’s real estate development world, Computer Vision is further enhanced by the capabilities of the drone technology. There is much area involved in large construction projects, and it would be time consuming and labor intensive to make detailed inspections on the ground. Available with high-resolution cameras, AI-powered drones can access rooftops, high ground, excavation sites, façades, bridges and other hard-to-reach places, and upload live footage to a monitoring centre. Computer Vision algorithms are used to analyse these images for structural defects, presence of unauthorised activity, equipment positioning, security concerns and potential safety issues. In addition to worker safety, drone monitoring also assists in documentation of the construction site, monitoring of works in progress, inventory management and quality control, allowing for multiple aspects of a project to be managed through a single intelligent drone platform. The integration will support in reducing the inspection cost, which will improve the operational efficiency and decision making process in the project lifecycle.
Predictive Safety Monitoring with Artificial Intelligence
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcTYHwe8RDjtUSQ4pO0FuR61vTEXd0XHvQNzPZtG06yrrg&s=10What Computer Vision can bring to the table is even more impressive when it comes to real-time monitoring in construction: the ability to prevent issues from becoming incidents. AI systems are no longer just about intelligence in surveillance; they’re quickly becoming predictive decision-support systems that can learn from thousands of images, videos, and historical project information. The application of Computer Vision and Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Internet of Things (IoT) sensors, Building Information Modeling (BIM), cloud computing and digital twins can enable construction companies to build a Safety Intelligence Ecosystem, with each camera, each sensor and each device contributing to a continuous flow of safety intelligence. Project managers are not waiting for an accident to occur, but are receiving warnings about unsafe patterns, repeat rule violations, equipment congestion, hazardous worker movement and changing environmental conditions, before they happen. The findings from the research undertaken and the examples from industry materials read throughout the literature are all in agreement that predictive monitoring is the next big thing in the construction safety management arena and is transforming the way that data is being turned into decisions, and that this is an important way to help mitigate injury, delay and operational uncertainty.
Computer Vision, Virtual Reality and Smart Safety Training
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcQXj6Y-FVzjnBcZH4qIfuvqu3nwL8NapLMOPjF5lHbrdA&s=10Yet another new direction that cropped up in all the advancements in the field is Computer Vision paired up with Virtual Reality (VR) and immersive safety training. The classroom training is very good for educating workers but unfortunately it is not adequate enough to provide training for the fast changing environments associated with active construction sites. VR technology with AI can simulate construction hazards for workers, so they don’t risk their safety. Employees can simulate fall response, equipment failure, electrical hazards, fire emergencies and confined spaces, crane operations, and emergency evacuation scenarios, learning and practicing, and then AI assesses their decisions and learning. Computer Vision also plays a role in this by monitoring the body posture, movements and reactions during training sessions, and aid an organisation in identifying skill gaps before the workers get to the construction site. The marriage of AI, VR, and visual analytics makes learning more engaging and effective, and ultimately lessens human error, one of the top causes of construction accidents.
Business Benefits for Builders and Developers
Improving Productivity, Compliance and Profitability
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcT4IbtiBNUy3KjVe1l_C6BMO3EYDoe-SVbZALocg66-dQ&s=10The Computer Vision can be used in other applications besides for the safety of the workers. It has a direct impact on project productivity, quality assurance, regulatory compliance and profitability for builders and developers. Each work accident has the potential to cost lives, livelihoods, compensation, legal fees, insurance, work stops production, costly equipment damage, delays with schedules and reputation. By leveraging AI monitoring, these risks can be reduced, as safety protocols are constantly adhered to. This enables the regulatory audit process and documentation to be streamlined, as it generates automated inspection reports, digital evidence, timestamped images and AI-generated incident logs, saving the administrator time. Construction companies no longer need to rely on manual reports, but can benefit from a transparent and objective, data-driven safety record that improves the transparency of the whole project process. This increases the efficiency of project teams to concentrate on the quality of construction, scheduling of resources and timely delivery of the projects.
Benefits for Real Estate Investors
https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcRLXE_OwUF__M4CIXiykCkBrE3GNz0WNg1IJTq_oeS2Xg&s=10As a real estate investor, you’ll be even more confident of your development projects thanks to Computer Vision. Incorporating tech
